title: Regime-wise OOF beta calibration and expert blending
summary: AUC models often benefit from blending differently biased experts, but uncalibrated specialists can over-dominate.
rationale: AUC models often benefit from blending differently biased experts, but uncalibrated specialists can over-dominate. Calibration literature suggests beta calibration is a stronger parametric fix than plain Platt scaling when score shapes are skewed, which is common for boosted trees and ranking models.
implementation_hint: Train 2-4 complementary experts only if they are genuinely different, for example a CatBoost categorical model, a ranking specialist, and the current heuristic-rich binary booster. Produce OOF predictions, then fit regime-specific beta calibration or isotonic-on-large-regimes for splits like wet vs slick, early/mid/late race, and low/high TyreLife. Blend only calibrated OOF predictions.
expected_effect: Turns diverse specialists into usable probabilities and may recover leaderboard gain even when single-model CV differences are small.
risk: Over-segmented calibration overfits fast; keep regimes coarse and fit calibrators strictly on OOF predictions only.
